codeReview.summary: The feature largely implements explicit runtime targeting, confirmation UX, duplicate-run protection, and transcript-confirmed success handling. However, the Room migration silently converts orphaned saved-host snippets into active-session snippets, which violates the feature requirement that stale targets must block for repair instead of being silently retargeted.

issues.description: The migration rewrites any snippet whose saved host no longer exists to `savedTarget = 'ACTIVE_SESSION'` while also nulling `hostId`. That silently retargets previously saved-host snippets to whatever live session exists after upgrade, instead of preserving the stale-target state and forcing repair. This contradicts both the feature requirement and the architecture invariant that snippet execution must never silently retarget when a saved target becomes stale or ambiguous.

summary: Reviewed commit e8e49209b0a7ba3362ebabc5763a166c21b66f83, the worker handoff, and the transcript skeleton. I found one blocking correctness issue: database migration logic silently retargets orphaned saved-host snippets to active-session execution, and the updated migration test currently asserts that incorrect behavior.
